title: OT Network Reconnaissance and Scanning
spl:
index=ot (sourcetype="ics:ids" OR sourcetype="ics:firewall" action="deny")
| bin _time span=5m
| stats dc(dest) as targets dc(dest_port) as ports count by _time, src
| where targets>=10 OR ports>=20
| lookup ot_asset_inventory src OUTPUT asset_owner zone
description: Detects port and service scanning inside ICS VLANs (T0846), often precursors to exploitation or lateral movement toward PLCs and HMIs.
value: Detects port and service scanning inside ICS VLANs (T0846), often precursors to exploitation or lateral movement toward PLCs and HMIs.
implementation: Use OT Security Add-on with ES. Distinguish vulnerability scanners from adversary behavior via source allow-lists. Map to T0846 in ES MITRE views.
visualization: Heatmap (time × src), Bubble chart (targets vs ports).
knownFalsePositives: Asset discovery, passive scanners, and legitimate vulnerability assessments generate recon-style noise — time-box with approved security testing windows.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: OT Security Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase 5151).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=ot` IDS/IPS `sourcetype="ics:ids"`, firewall deny logs.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Use OT Security Add-on with ES. Distinguish vulnerability scanners from adversary behavior via source allow-lists. Map to T0846 in ES MITRE views.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ot (sourcetype="ics:ids" OR sourcetype="ics:firewall" action="deny")
| bin _time span=5m
| stats dc(dest) as targets dc(dest_port) as ports count by _time, src
| where targets>=10 OR ports>=20
| lookup ot_asset_inventory src OUTPUT asset_owner zone
```

Understanding this SPL

**OT Network Reconnaissance and Scanning** — Detects port and service scanning inside ICS VLANs (T0846), often precursors to exploitation or lateral movement toward PLCs and HMIs.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=ot` IDS/IPS `sourcetype="ics:ids"`, firewall deny logs. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): OT Security Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase 5151). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ot; **sourcetype**: ics:ids, ics:firewall. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=ot, sourcetype="ics:ids". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Discretizes time or numeric ranges with `bin`/`bucket`.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by _time, src** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Filters the current rows with `where targets>=10 OR ports>=20` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).

Step 3 — Validate
Compare recon spikes to the **OT Security** / IDS view and any **scheduled vulnerability** scan that day so friendly scanning does not look like a threat actor.



Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Heatmap (time × src), Bubble chart (targets vs ports).
grandmaExplanation: We notice when someone is mapping or poking the OT network the way a thief cases a building before a break-in.
